Bitcoin price may rally post US debt suspension end. BTC dropped to $82,535 from $106,000 during period. Liquidity influx expected to boost markets. Analysts predict $132,000 price by 2025. Trading chart and expert insights included.
news.summary: The United States debt suspension period is set to end on March 14, injecting fresh liquidity into markets and potentially driving a rebound in the price of Bitcoin. Bitcoin has dropped 22% during the two-month debt suspension plan, falling from over $106,000 on Jan. 21 to $82,535 on March 12. The resumption of government spending is expected to boost liquidity and catalyze Bitcoin's next rally. However, concerns such as inflation, interest rates, and geopolitical issues remain unresolved. Some analysts predict a potential correction in Bitcoin's price to near $70,000 by the end of the debt suspension period. Despite short-term concerns, price predictions for late 2025 range from $160,000 to above $180,000. Global trade war concerns and retaliatory tariffs may impact Bitcoin’s price in the short term. The end of the US debt suspension period is anticipated to drive market price movements but its long-term trajectory remains tied to institutional investments, ETF growth, and regulatory clarity.

news.summary: On March 12, 2025, LPL Financial LLC welcomed financial advisors James Larsen, CFP®, Lucas Decker, CFP®, Kevin Lewis, CFP®, CEPA®, and Seth Miller, CFP®, CEPA®, to its broker-dealer, Registered Investment Advisor (RIA), and custodial platforms. These advisors, previously with Wells Fargo Advisors and Edward Jones, have now aligned with Carnegie Private Wealth, a firm supported by LPL Strategic Wealth. news.headline: Bank of Russia may permit select investors to trade crypto, requiring $1.1M in assets. Bans retail crypto payments in Russia.
news.summary: The Russian central bank is considering a new experimental regime to allow select investors to trade cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. Investors with at least $1.1 million in securities and deposits could be authorized under this proposal, which also suggests penalties for unauthorized crypto transactions. Despite this, retail crypto payments remain strictly prohibited in Russia under the current law, "On Digital Financial Assets," which came into effect in January 2021. The central bank views cryptocurrency as an investment rather than a means of payment. Residents are not allowed to make payments using cryptocurrency, and the bank proposes to ban settlements between residents involving cryptocurrencies. Additional details on this developing situation will be provided as they emerge.

news.headline: Remedy Cell partners with Qureight to introduce AI in Phase 1b study of RC-0315 for IPF treatment.
news.summary: On March 12, 2025, HAIFA, Israel, and CAMBRIDGE, England saw the collaboration of Remedy Cell and Qureight Ltd in the field of biopharmaceuticals. Remedy Cell, a leading company in clinical-stage biopharmaceuticals, specializes in developing cell-derived therapies for fibrotic diseases. On the other hand, Qureight Ltd is a Core Imaging Laboratory with expertise in deep-learning analytics. This partnership aims to combine Remedy Cell's innovative therapies with Qureight Ltd's advanced analytics to enhance the treatment and understanding of fibrotic diseases. The collaboration is set to revolutionize the industry and potentially benefit millions of patients worldwide.

Critical Metals Corp. releases S-K 1300 report on Tanbreez 4.7B metric ton Kakortokite Host Rock, with maiden mineral resource estimate of 45M metric tons at 0.4% TREO, incl. ~27% Heavy REEs.
news.summary: The recent mineral resource estimate reveals a significant discovery of 45 million metric tons of resources containing 0.4% Total Rare Earth Oxides (TREO). Notably, about 27% of the TREO consists of Heavy Rare Earth Elements. This substantial find is situated within the world's largest known rare earth hard rock deposit.

news.headline: Diamond Properties celebrates 30 years with major acquisitions, expanding portfolio by adding 1.4M sq ft of commercial space.
news.summary: Diamond Properties, based in Mount Kisco, New York, commemorates its 30th anniversary with a focus on visionary investments and continuous growth in commercial real estate. The company has expanded its portfolio in the past year through strategic acquisitions in Ohio, North Carolina, Indianapolis, and Florida, as well as gaining three more properties at the end of 2023. This recent expansion has increased Diamond Properties' commercial space by 1.4 million square feet.

news.headline: iRobot stock plunges after Q4 FY24 earnings report: Sales down 44% to $172.03M, gross profit drops 71% to $16.4M. EPS loss $2.06.
news.summary: In premarket trading, iRobot Corp (NASDAQ:IRBT) shares are down following their fourth-quarter FY24 earnings report. The company saw a 44% year-on-year decrease in fourth-quarter sales to $172.03 million, surpassing the analyst consensus of $171.00 million. Their adjusted EPS loss was $2.06, missing the estimated loss of $1.73. The quarter's gross margin shrunk by 940 basis points to 9.4%, resulting in a 71% decline in gross profit to $16.4 million. Operating expenses for the quarter dropped by 30% to $77.5 million, leading to an expanded operating loss of $61 million. The company's cash reserves as of December 28, 2024, were not disclosed.

news.headline: Casey's General Stores achieves record highs in Q3 FY24 with $3.90B revenue, 3.7% same-store sales growth, and $302.1M fuel gross profit.
news.summary: Shares of Caseys General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) are showing a boost in premarket trading following the release of their third-quarter FY24 results. The company reported a revenue of $3.90 billion, surpassing the expected $3.73 billion. Same-store sales saw a 3.7% increase year-over-year and an 8.0% increase over two years, with an inside margin of 40.9%. The growth was primarily due to strong performance in prepared food and dispensed beverage categories, specifically with hot sandwiches and bakery items. Same-store fuel volumes also went up by 1.8% Y/Y, with a fuel margin of 36.4 cents per gallon. Total inside gross profit experienced a 14.3% increase Y/Y to $573.1 million, while fuel gross profit rose by 17.4% Y/Y to $302.1 million. EBITDA saw an 11.4% rise Y/Y to $242.4 million, driven by improved inside performance.
